page: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by two Intel Xeon 8175M processors. It features 96 virtual CPUs (48 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 3.1 GHz, supported by 384 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 3,600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 82,016.64 and a Passmark CPU score of 56,893.67. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4,545.24 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 216.75 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The server demonstrates high throughput in database operations, achieving 76.56 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and excels in static web serving at 48.67 GB/sec.

While the instance delivers strong performance in software compilation and database operations, it exhibits limitations in machine learning tasks. Specifically, LLM text generation speeds are poor, yielding only 5.11 tokens per second on a medium 7B model, though prompt processing speeds remain average. Given these characteristics, the m5d.metal is highly suited for large-scale databases, enterprise general-purpose applications, web serving, and software development pipelines, but is not recommended for active LLM text generation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical Intel Xeon 8175M cores) running at 3.1 GHz, 384 GB of DDR4 memory, and 3,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 56,893.67 and a Redis SET throughput of 76.56 million operations per second. While memory bandwidth and static web serving (48.67 GB/sec) are highly rated, the server exhibits poor performance in LLM text generation tasks. This bare-metal instance offers high resource density and is optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3,600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3,600 GB NVMe storage for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal instance combining high-core Intel Xeon compute with fast local NVMe storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM on a dedicated bare-metal architecture
3,600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput memory bandwidth
Excellent fit for large-scale databases, web serving, and software compilation
Poor performance for LLM text generation workloads
